appeal_letter:
Dear Cigna Appeals Review,

I am writing to appeal the denial of Orthognathic Surgery (Bilateral Sagittal Split Osteotomy) for [Member Name], dated [date]. The denial categorizes this surgery as cosmetic because the submitted records document difficulty chewing and jaw pain without meeting Cigna's threshold of documented malnutrition or significant weight loss. This appeal asks you to review the complete clinical record, which documents functional impairment that satisfies Cigna's own criteria.

The patient has a severe underbite causing chronic temporomandibular joint (TMJ) dysfunction and daily headaches. As a direct result of the difficulty chewing solid foods, the patient has adopted a predominantly liquid diet. The treating provider can document the dietary restriction and the associated weight and nutritional history, which were not included in the initial submission.

Cigna's Medical Coverage Policy recognizes orthognathic surgery as medically necessary when there is an inability to chew or swallow that has caused documented weight loss. The patient's liquid diet and the documented inability to consume solid food constitute the functional impairment Cigna's criteria require. The omission of weight logs and dietary documentation from the initial submission was an administrative gap, not an absence of the underlying clinical facts.

We ask that the reviewer consider the attached supplemental documentation — including the treating provider's dietary assessment, weight history, and a declaration that the restricted diet is a direct consequence of the structural jaw condition — before upholding the cosmetic classification.

We request a clinical review and reversal. Please confirm receipt and the deadline for any further submission.

This letter is a draft for your review.

feature_scores.evidence: Quotes Cigna's functional impairment criteria and maps the patient's presentation directly to that language.
Correctly identifies the vector: documentation gap, not absence of the underlying clinical condition.
Dietary restriction and liquid diet noted but weight history not yet provided — flagged as the key missing evidence.

gaps: moderate: case_specific_clinical_rebuttal (anchor 3) — supplemental weight history and dietary documentation are the linchpin of the reversal
outcome.critique: Strong framing that recharacterizes the denial as a documentation gap; the outcome depends on the supplemental records.
rationale: Cigna's criteria for functional impairment are met on the facts; the initial submission just did not document them.
Liquid diet as a documented consequence of jaw structure directly addresses the 'cosmetic' classification.

risk_flags: Reversal depends on providing the supplemental weight and dietary documentation — without those records, the appeal may not succeed.

mirror.why_they_said_no: They say the surgery is cosmetic and not medically necessary — the submitted records only show difficulty chewing and jaw pain, which Cigna says does not reach the threshold for functional impairment.
mirror.deadline_note: Check your denial letter for the appeal filing deadline and submit before that date.
mirror.strongest_angle: The patient is eating only liquids because of the jaw condition — that is exactly the functional impairment Cigna's own policy requires, and it was simply not documented in the first submission.

citations_used.title: Cigna Medical Coverage Policy (referenced in denial)
citations_used.quote: inability to chew/swallow that has caused documented weight loss

citations_used.title: Denial letter
citations_used.quote: records provided only note difficulty chewing and jaw pain, which does not meet the threshold for functional impairment

missing_evidence_checklist: Weight history documenting loss or restricted intake linked to the jaw condition
Treating provider's dietary assessment and letter confirming the liquid diet is a result of the structural problem
TMJ evaluation documenting chronic pain and its functional consequences
